Role / Description: Public sources reviewed do not identify the specific DDG-51 item or service. Portuguese business directories describe SOCAIL as an industrial-piping/plumbing and building-construction contractor; because no reliable source ties a particular product or work package to the DDG subaward, the DDG-51 role cannot be confirmed.
NAICS-6 title: Not confirmed — foreign entity; no primary U.S. NAICS located.

Role / Description: Supplies Integrated Bridge and Navigation System (IBNS) steering-control hardware for DDG-51-class ships; a Navy procurement specifically identifies an IBNS aft steering unit.

Provides marine fire-protection equipment and engineering. A Hiller-approved 1½-inch stop-check valve is specifically identified as a component of the Halon fire-extinguishing system on DDG-51 through DDG-58; Hiller also designs, installs, and services shipboard detection and suppression systems.
Supplies solid-state frequency/power-conversion equipment and related power-distribution magnetics. Its ACFC-288 converts 60-Hz ship-service power to 400 Hz for AEGIS equipment on DDG-51-class ships.
NAICS-6 title: Power, Distribution, and Specialty Transformer Manufacturing

Role / Description: Supplies retractable mooring bitts for DDG-51 construction. The Navy identified Entwistle as the original-equipment manufacturer and sole authorized manufacturer for these long-lead deck-machinery items.
Supplies centrifugal pumps and OEM parts/overhaul support for DDG-51 shipboard fluid systems. A Navy solicitation identifies Buffalo DH6080D-series centrifugal pumps for the Arleigh Burke class.

Supplies heat-recovery evaporators/fresh-water generators that desalinate seawater into potable water. DDG-51 construction subawards cite the Maxim evaporator purchase specification, although public records do not expose the exact commercial model.

Supplies self-cleaning disk-stack centrifugal fuel purifiers used to remove water and solids from DDG-51 fuel-transfer streams. The DDG subaward is recorded under fuel-system purchase specification 541-10B.

Supplies Blackmer rotary/hand pumps and related pump hardware for DDG-51. Public Navy item data also identifies a Blackmer relief-valve kit for the class’s aqueous-film-forming-foam firefighting pump.
Supplies STAR-200 high-pressure air compressors for DDG-51 Flight III ships, supporting shipboard compressed-air requirements.

The DDG-128/129 construction subaward is labeled “TMR rebuy ES012 10451,” but the public record does not expand TMR or identify the exact equipment. EMS manufactures naval power-conversion/degaussing electronics, transformers, and power supplies; a specific product-to-subaward match could not be confirmed.
Supplies shipboard butterfly/gate valve assemblies and replacement parts for DDG-51 piping systems. Public NSN data identifies Morpac butterfly valves with the Arleigh Burke class as an end item.
Supplies Navy-qualified gate, globe, check/swing-check, and ball valves for DDG-51 fluid piping. Public item records identify Milwaukee swing-check valves specifically used on the class.

Performs welding/fabrication, ship-repair, and warranty work on DDG-51-class ships. Public records tie Southcoast to DDG construction warranty support and to repair work on USS Fitzgerald (DDG-62), but do not disclose a specific construction component in the listed subaward.

Supplies/distributes and repairs naval valve and actuator hardware under the J.A. Moody name. Public item data links an ESI valve assembly to the Arleigh Burke class, but the exact valve or actuator covered by this DDG construction subaward is not publicly identified.
Supplies electrical-distribution hardware, including circuit breakers for DDG-51 weapons systems and shore-power cable adapters used on the class.
Supplies engineered fluid-control valves and manifolds for DDG-51 shipboard systems; Marotta identifies itself as a major valve/manifold supplier to the class.

The DDG subaward is under electrical/communications purchase specification 431-01C. Dynalec manufactures rugged shipboard communications, navigation, and switching equipment—especially sound-powered telephone/communications systems—but the exact unit in this subaward is not publicly disclosed.

Supplies automatic transfer-switch/power-control assemblies and electronic modules for shipboard electrical distribution. DDG subawards are documented, and Bogue ATS-4100 modules are publicly cataloged; the exact model purchased under the cited DDG line is not disclosed.

Supplies naval motors, motor controllers, control components, heaters, overload relays, and systems-integration support. DDG-51 technical records identify Ward Leonard motor-controller equipment installed on the class; the specific construction-line item is not public.
Provided fuel for DDG-125 construction, testing, and/or sea-trial support; the subcontract description explicitly identifies fuel for the ship.
Supplies deck-machinery hardware, including the emergency quick-disconnect switch and weldment handle for the DDG-51 slewing-arm davit (SLAD).
Supplies air-moisture separation and filtration systems for DDG-51 gas-turbine air intakes, removing water droplets and contaminants before air enters the engines.

Supplies Cla-Val automatic-control, pressure-reducing, and solenoid valve assemblies/components for DDG-51 fluid systems. Public item records identify Griswold/Cla-Val valve parts with the Arleigh Burke class as an end item, but the exact valve model in this subaward is not public.

Supplies Model 6995 low-pressure membrane compressed-air dehydrators selected for DDG-51 new construction and Model 7600 ECOLCELL electrochemical anti-fouling chlorinators, class-standard beginning with DDG-103.

Supplies marine-interior/joiner outfitting—steel/aluminum furniture, accommodation fittings, and related interior packages. A NIST shipbuilding-industry report identifies Jamestown as a marine-interiors outfitter, but public sources do not enumerate the exact items in this DDG subaward.

Juniper manufactures Navy watertight, ballistic, and weathertight doors, hatches, scuttles/manholes, plus marine-ventilation components. Public sources reviewed do not identify which of these products was supplied under this DDG-51 subaward.
Manufactures and repairs waterborne-propulsion shafting—propeller shafts, intermediate shafts, and stern-tube shafts—for DDG-51 ships.

Supplies low-smoke, flame-retardant shipboard electrical/fiber-optic cable and cable assemblies under the RSCC/Marmon brands. A DDG-51 construction subcontract is documented, but public records do not disclose the exact cable type or quantity.

Supplies naval pumps and pump replacement/aftermarket components from the Warren and IMO product families for DDG-51 fluid systems. CIRCOR’s Warren/Simsite page explicitly lists DDG-51 among supported applications; the exact subaward pump model is not public.

Supplies fuel immersion/flange heaters and associated temperature-sensing/control hardware for DDG-51. Public NSN data identifies an INDEECO thermocouple used on the class’s fuel immersion-flange heater.

Performs abrasive blasting, surface preparation, and protective marine coating/painting on DDG construction. Public sources confirm its naval preservation/coatings capability, but the exact DDG compartment or coating system in the listed subaward is not disclosed.
Supplies shock-resistant vacuum toilets, flush units, blackwater collection equipment, and sewage-pumping hardware for DDG-51 sanitary systems.
Supplies and installs interior deck coverings/flooring on DDG-51 ships; a DDG construction subcontract is explicitly described as “deck covering.”

The DDG-128 subaward is a rebuy under ES012 11250, but public records do not identify the component. Facet/Filtration Group makes fuel-filtration, water-separation, and coalescer equipment; no reliable source was found matching a particular Facet model to this DDG line, so the exact role cannot be confirmed.
Supplies ionization smoke detectors for DDG-51 fire/alarm systems; the Navy identifies Meggitt P/N SAS-04398-01 as the sole approved source for the class.
Performs maintenance, repair, alterations, and fabrication on DDG-51-class ships during availabilities. Public records confirm DDG-class repair work but do not specify a new-construction component.

Supplies hydrodynamic thrust and journal bearings for DDG-51 propulsion shaft lines and rotating machinery. Public item data links a Kingsbury bearing-related assembly to the Arleigh Burke class, but the exact bearing set in this construction subaward is not disclosed.
Supplies tank liquid-level/quantity sensors, transmitters, receivers, and indicators for DDG-51 shipboard tanks and monitoring systems.

A DDG-51 subcontract is recorded for this UEI, but public sources reviewed do not identify the delivered item or work package. SteelFab is a structural-steel fabricator; without a source connecting a specific fabricated assembly to the DDG subaward, the role cannot be confirmed.

Supplies slewing-arm davits and deck machinery used to launch and recover small boats. Appleton is a Navy-contracted SLAD supplier; public sources reviewed do not identify the exact davit model or hull number covered by this listed DDG construction subaward.
Supplies marine ball valves and inline/twinline check valves for DDG-51 piping systems; BNL’s own catalog lists DDG-51 among supported Navy ship classes.

Supplies shipboard electrical wire and cable for DDG-51 construction, including low-smoke/zero-halogen Navy cable families such as MIL-DTL-24643, MIL-DTL-24640, and MIL-DTL-915. The DDG subcontract description is simply “Cable,” so the exact specification and circuit are not public.
Supplies sediment-strainer elements and automatic backflushing strainers for DDG-51 fluid systems; NSN 4730-01-475-2135 identifies the Arleigh Burke class as an end product.

Distributes marine valves, pipe/tube, fittings, strainers/filtration, and bulkhead-sealing products for DDG construction. Public item data links W&O-supplied demineralizer components to the Arleigh Burke class, but the exact material mix in this subaward is not disclosed.

Supplies forged Navy anchor chain and detachable connecting links/deck chain fittings for DDG-51 anchoring and mooring systems. The current DDG subcontract is documented; public sources do not disclose the chain size or grade.

Supplies automatic bus-transfer-switch assemblies and internal electrical parts, including power transformers and switch components, used in DDG-51 shipboard distribution. Public NSN records identify ASCO ABT components for the class.

Performs ship preservation—abrasive blasting, surface preparation, and application/removal of protective coatings—plus structural repair support for Navy vessels. Public sources do not identify the specific DDG-51 compartment or coating work package in this subaward.

Supplies nonpowered shipboard valves—such as globe/stop/check and relief-valve assemblies and replacement parts—for DDG-51 fluid systems. Public NSN data identifies multiple Dante valve models with the Arleigh Burke class as the end item.

Supplies the DDG-51 class slewing-arm boat-davit system and associated replacement/overhaul kits. A Navy award for USS Stockdale (DDG-106) identifies Lake Shore as the OEM for the installed slewing-arm davit, part number 56W4-S100.

Supplies propulsion bearing systems for Arleigh Burke-class destroyers. AMB lists the class among vessels equipped with its systems; its naval product line includes line-shaft, stern-tube, strut, and thrust bearings.

Through Parker Village Marine, supplies MIL-SPEC reverse-osmosis desalination and water-purification systems installed on Arleigh Burke-class destroyers. The systems produce potable water and high-purity permeate for shipboard use.
NAICS-6 title: Commercial and Service Industry Machinery Manufacturing

Role / Description: Through its HAF Navy Market/Howden American Fan business, supplied DDG-51 cooling and ventilation fans, including gas-turbine-room blowers, collective-protection-system fans, and vane-axial/centrifugal fans. Fairbanks Morse Defense acquired American Fan in 2023 and now supplies the same product line on Flight III destroyers.
NAICS-6 title: Industrial and Commercial Fan and Blower and Air Purification Equipment Manufacturing

Role / Description: Supplies the DDG-51 helicopter-hangar door system and replacement components. Jered’s electrically driven folding NAVTRUSS-panel doors are installed on the class and are qualified to Navy Grade A shock requirements.

Supplies DDG-51 shipboard chilled-water air-conditioning plants, including 50-ton HFC-134a High-Efficiency Super-Capacity chillers. The Navy procures these plants to meet added cooling demand from DDG-51 electronic-warfare upgrades.
NAICS-6 title: Air-Conditioning and Warm Air Heating Equipment and Commercial and Industrial Refrigeration Equipment Manufacturing

Role / Description: This UEI is Rolls-Royce Naval Marine’s Walpole, Massachusetts operation. For DDG-51 ships, public Rolls-Royce evidence ties Walpole to controllable-pitch-propeller hub and oil-distribution-box work and to production/service of naval propeller components; it should not be conflated with the Indianapolis sibling that supplies the generator-set gas-turbine prime movers.
NAICS-6 title: All Other Miscellaneous Fabricated Metal Product Manufacturing

Role / Description: This UEI is the MTU/Rolls-Royce Solutions America diesel-engine business in Novi, Michigan. I found no public evidence assigning this entity a distinct DDG-51 new-construction component; Rolls-Royce’s documented DDG generator sets use 501-K34 or MT5S gas turbines supplied through a different Rolls-Royce entity.

The Indianapolis Rolls-Royce Corporation entity supplies the gas-turbine prime movers used in DDG-51 ship-service generator sets: the 501-K34 in the AG9140 and the MT5S in the Flight III AG9160. This is the engine-manufacturing role, distinct from Walpole’s propeller/propulsor work.

GE’s Evendale/Cincinnati aircraft-engine entity produces LM2500 aeroderivative gas turbines used as the DDG-51 class’s main-propulsion engines. GE states that each Arleigh Burke-class destroyer is powered by four LM2500-family engines.
NAICS-6 title: Aircraft Engine and Engine Parts Manufacturing

Role / Description: This UEI is GE Energy Power Conversion’s Pennsylvania operation, whose naval portfolio covers shipboard power conversion, distribution, drives, automation, and controls. Public sources do not identify the exact DDG-51 hardware represented by this subaward, so a more specific component cannot be confirmed.

Panametrics manufactures process-instrumentation products such as ultrasonic flowmeters and moisture, oxygen, and gas analyzers. Navy records show this exact UEI supplying measurement equipment, including dew-point indicators, but I found no public source tying a particular Panametrics instrument to DDG-51 construction.
NAICS-6 title: Instruments and Related Products Manufacturing for Measuring, Displaying, and Controlling Industrial Process Variables

Role / Description: This legacy York International registration is the York, Pennsylvania HVAC/refrigeration operation. YORK equipment is used for shipboard heating, ventilation, air-conditioning, and refrigeration, but public evidence does not separate this UEI’s DDG-51 deliverable from the affiliated Johnson Controls Navy Systems entity.
NAICS-6 title: Air-Conditioning and Warm Air Heating Equipment and Commercial and Industrial Refrigeration Equipment Manufacturing

Role / Description: The Marinette, Wisconsin Tyco Fire Products entity manufactures fire-suppression equipment and agents, including valves, nozzles, sprinklers, and foam-system products. Its exact DDG-51 shipset item is not publicly identified, so the role can only be confirmed at the fire-protection-system component level.

Air Systems Components’ Sanford, North Carolina operation includes the Trion air-cleaning business, which makes electrostatic air cleaners, filtration units, and related indoor-air-quality equipment. I found no public source identifying the specific Trion/Air Systems component installed during DDG-51 construction.
NAICS-6 title: Industrial and Commercial Fan and Blower and Air Purification Equipment Manufacturing

Role / Description: This is a legacy Tyco Fire Products Limited Partnership registration associated with fire-protection equipment. The exact entity-level DDG-51 component—and even the continuing operating site behind this UEI—could not be confirmed from public sources, so it should not inherit the Marinette sibling’s detailed product description.
NAICS-6 title: All Other Miscellaneous General Purpose Machinery Manufacturing

Role / Description: The Menomonee Falls DRS Naval Power Systems entity manufactures naval electrical power-distribution hardware, including transformers, switchboards/load centers, power-conditioning equipment, and related controls. Public sources confirm the facility and product family, but do not disclose the exact DDG-51 unit covered by this UEI’s subaward.
NAICS-6 title: Power, Distribution, and Specialty Transformer Manufacturing

Role / Description: Engineered Coil Company is Leonardo DRS’s Marlo Coil operation in High Ridge, Missouri. It manufactures custom Navy and marine heat-transfer equipment—evaporator and condenser coils, air coolers, and other HVAC/refrigeration coils—for shipboard environmental-control systems; the public record does not identify the exact DDG-51 coil assembly.
NAICS-6 title: Air-Conditioning and Warm Air Heating Equipment and Commercial and Industrial Refrigeration Equipment Manufacturing

Role / Description: Pivotal Power’s Bedford, Nova Scotia entity makes rugged military power-conversion and power-supply equipment. A Navy award to this exact UEI identifies a shipboard power supply for USS Decatur (DDG-73), providing a concrete DDG-51 application.
NAICS-6 title: All Other Miscellaneous Electrical Equipment and Component Manufacturing

Role / Description: The Fort Walton Beach DRS Training & Control Systems entity develops military training, control, communications, and wireless-electronics products. I found no public evidence identifying a discrete DDG-51 construction component supplied by this UEI.
NAICS-6 title: Radio and Television Broadcasting and Wireless Communications Equipment Manufacturing

Role / Description: DRS Technologies Canada in Ottawa produces naval communications equipment such as secure shipboard terminals, telephones, headsets, and associated electronics. Public award data supports those product lines for this UEI, but the exact DDG-51 installation represented by the subaward is not disclosed.
NAICS-6 title: Radio and Television Broadcasting and Wireless Communications Equipment Manufacturing

Role / Description: DRS Laurel Technologies in Johnstown, Pennsylvania supplies Aegis combat-system electronics, including TI-24 consoles/displays and Mk 41 Vertical Launching System launch-control and power-supply modules. Those are concrete combat-system construction items distinct from DRS’s propulsion-power and HVAC-coil businesses.
NAICS-6 title: Search, Detection, Navigation, Guidance, Aeronautical, and Nautical System and Instrument Manufacturing

Role / Description: This UEI is the Tulsa maritime power business formerly known as L-3 Westwood. It supplies MIL-SPEC shipboard switchboards and load centers used to distribute electrical power on DDG-51-class ships.

The Anaheim L3Harris Maritime Power & Energy Solutions entity manufactures naval power and distribution equipment, with its registered industry focused on specialty transformers. Public sources do not isolate the exact transformer or power package supplied to the DDG-51 program under this UEI.
NAICS-6 title: Power, Distribution, and Specialty Transformer Manufacturing

Role / Description: Ellwood National Forge’s Irvine, Pennsylvania operation produces large open-die steel forgings and forged shafting for naval and other heavy-duty applications. The specific DDG-51 forging covered by this subaward is not publicly identified, so no particular shaft, rotor, or coupling should be assigned.

Despite the workbook label, this UEI resolves to Ellwood City Forge Company, a separate Ellwood plant that makes large open-die forgings, shafts, rotors, and other heavy steel components. I found no public source identifying which DDG-51 forging this site supplied.

SPD Electrical Systems in Camden, New Jersey manufactures shock-hardened naval circuit breakers, switchgear, and power-distribution equipment. Its DDG-51 role is electrical protection and distribution hardware, distinct from L3Harris’s Tulsa switchboard assembly and Anaheim transformer businesses.

This UEI is L3Harris Henschel’s Norfolk ship-control business. It supplies machinery-control and monitoring equipment—operator consoles, control panels, alarms, and associated shipboard control electronics—for naval vessels; public sources do not itemize the exact DDG-51 cabinet or console in this subaward.

This UEI resolves to the former L-3 Security & Detection Systems business, now Leidos Security Detection & Automation in Tewksbury, Massachusetts. Its public product line is airport/cargo security screening, and I found no credible evidence of a DDG-51 ship-construction role for this exact entity.

ADI Technologies is a procurement/technical-services company rather than a ship-component manufacturer. Navy notices tied to this UEI show it furnishing a quick-acting watertight door for USS Preble (DDG-88), so its concrete role is sourcing that closure assembly rather than manufacturing the broader parent’s interior systems.

US Joiner fabricates and installs naval joiner systems such as non-structural bulkheads, doors, modular berths/furnishings, and fire-rated interior barriers. These are ship-interior and habitability construction products, distinct from Trident’s HVAC and IMECO’s machinery/electrical integration work.

Aircraft Appliances and Equipment Limited manufactures military pressure switches, control panels, power panels, and related electromechanical equipment. Public Navy notices confirm those product types for this entity, but do not identify the precise DDG-51 assembly represented by the subaward.

IMECO’s Marinette operation engineers and integrates marine HVAC, electrical, and machinery packages. A Navy notice linked to this UEI identifies a motor-and-brake assembly, but public sources do not disclose which DDG-51 shipboard system the subaward supported.
NAICS-6 title: Air-Conditioning and Warm Air Heating Equipment and Commercial and Industrial Refrigeration Equipment Manufacturing

Role / Description: Trident Maritime Systems is the corporate/integration entity for marine HVAC, refrigeration, power, automation, and ship-interior packages. Public procurement records connect this UEI to marine HVAC/Johnson Controls equipment, but the exact DDG-51 equipment package cannot be distinguished from work performed by its operating subsidiaries.

This Chesapeake, Virginia Wartsila Defense entity is the U.S. engineering/manufacturing operation for naval shaft-line and propulsion products, including propellers and shaft, rudder, and stern-tube seals. Public sources support those product families, but do not identify the exact DDG-51 new-construction item in this subaward.

This UEI is Wartsila/Defense Maritime Solutions’ Poulsbo, Washington registration, separate from the Chesapeake manufacturing entity. Its public footprint is West Coast installation, repair, overhaul, and parts support for naval shaft-line, propeller, and seal systems; an exact DDG-51 new-construction component was not publicly confirmed.

Portland Valve manufactures Navy ship-service ball valves and other specialty valves used on destroyers, submarines, and carriers. For DDG-51 construction, this entity’s distinct contribution is valve hardware for shipboard fluid systems, although the exact valve list is not public.

Leslie Controls manufactures severe-service regulating and control valves, pressure-reducing valves, and fluid-control systems. Its DDG-51 role is shipboard control-valve hardware; the public record does not identify the particular valve or service supplied under this UEI.

This UEI is CIRCOR Aerospace’s Corona, California operation, not a CIRCOR naval-valve or pump business. I found no public evidence tying this aerospace-focused entity to a distinct DDG-51 construction component; a Navy notice for the UEI concerns sanitary-tank hardware but does not establish a DDG-51 shipset role.
NAICS-6 title: Aircraft Engine and Engine Parts Manufacturing

Role / Description: L3Harris Maritime Services in Norfolk provides shipyard production labor and installation services, including welding, pipefitting, shipfitting, blasting/painting, and equipment installation. Public sources do not disclose the exact DDG-51 work package, but this is a hands-on construction/service role rather than an equipment-manufacturing role.

This UEI is L3’s Communications Systems-West operation in Salt Lake City, a manufacturer of secure tactical communications electronics. I found no public evidence identifying a distinct DDG-51 construction item supplied by this entity.
NAICS-6 title: Radio and Television Broadcasting and Wireless Communications Equipment Manufacturing
